title: Uniface Component Types
text: Uniface Component Types Uniface components handle most of the processing of a Uniface application. The different types of Uniface component are intended for different platforms and serve different functions. Note: All components share the same component namespace, so component names must be unique, regardless of the type of component. For example, it is not possible to have a Dynamic Server Page and a Form called PURCHASE_ORDER. Components for the user interface of web and mobile apps: Dynamic Server Pages —interactive pages for incrementally and dynamically displaying and updating data in a web or mobile environment. Static Server Pages —interactive pages for displaying and updating data in a web environment. The web pages are processed as complete pages. Components for the presentation layer of desktop applications: Forms —interactive screens for displaying and updating data in a desktop environment. Reports —layouts for presenting data in a printed output. Components for the business logic and data layers: Services —components without a user interface that contain operations that can be used locally or remotely. Session Services —service components that manage task-specific behavior, complex business behavior, transactions, and referential integrity. Session services centralize complex business rules affecting multiple data entities. Entity Services —service components that centralize simple business rules for single data entities. Related Links Component A Component is a Main Development Object that defines an executable part of an application. It defines the data structure used to access data, the layout (if applicable) used to present it, and the actions used to modify, validate, and store data. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
